issues.display_title: Issue #179: Fix splash_screens nesting in CreateExhibition (move to exhibition root, drop from published_data)
issues.main_description_html:
RESULT 17/06/26

✅ Implementation Complete — Ready for Unit Testing

Overview

Correct the data-structure nesting for splash_screens in the CreateExhibition action. The previous implementation incorrectly nested splash_screens inside published_data, causing inconsistency with frontend expectations and other viewer-related endpoints. This task moves splash_screens to the root of the exhibition object in viewer.json, keeps it out of the published_data DB column, and routes payload generation through RebuildViewerPayload for a single source of truth consistent with getViewerJSON.go.

Scope of Changes

src/action/v1/exhibition/createExhibition.go

  • buildPublishedData
    • Remove the splashSettings parameter from the signature.
    • Remove logic injecting splash_screens into the returned JSON.
    • Return pure exhibition data for the initial published_data column.
  • CreateExhibition
    • Update the user_detail query to fetch json_version.
    • Move splashSettings initialization inside the transaction so the correct exhibitionID is used.
    • Replace manual viewer.json payload generation with a call to RebuildViewerPayload.
    • Update published_data in the DB with the final base64 string from RebuildViewerPayload.

Acceptance Criteria

  • viewer.json has exhibition.splash_screens at the exhibition root (sibling of id, name, user).
  • published_data JSON column does not contain splash_screens.
  • Structure matches getViewerJSON.go (byte-consistent payload).
  • buildPublishedData no longer accepts splashSettings.
  • user_detail query fetches json_version.
  • splashSettings initialized inside the transaction with the correct exhibitionID.
  • published_data column stores the base64 string from RebuildViewerPayload.
  • Package src/action/v1/exhibition compiles successfully (go build ./...).

Final Data Structure (viewer.json)

{
  "model_size": "...",
  "user_data": { "avatar": "...", "username": "...", "first_name": "...", "last_name": "..." },
  "exhibition": {
    "id": "...",
    "name": "...",
    "splash_screens": { "image": "", "header": "", "button_text": "Enter Gallery" },
    "user": { "website_url": "...", "facebook_url": "...", "instagram_url": "...", "user_details": [] }
  }
}

🧪 Scenario Testing (for Tester)

Scenario A — splash_screens placement

  1. Create a new exhibition via the CreateExhibition endpoint.
  2. Fetch the generated viewer.json.
  3. Verify splash_screens appears under exhibition (sibling of id, name, user), not under published_data.
  4. Confirm default values render (e.g. button_text: "Enter Gallery", empty image/header).

Scenario B — published_data is clean

  1. Decode the published_data base64 column from the DB for the new exhibition.
  2. Confirm it is valid base64 and decodes to consistent JSON.
  3. Verify splash_screens is absent from published_data.
  4. Confirm no other previously-present published_data fields were lost (no regression).

Scenario C — single source of truth & versioning

  1. Capture both viewer.json and published_data for the same exhibition.
  2. Verify the viewer payload is byte-consistent with getViewerJSON.go output for that exhibition.
  3. Verify json_version is correctly populated from user_detail.
  4. Create exhibitions for users with different json_version values and confirm correct versioning.

Scenario D — correct exhibitionID in splash settings

  1. Create an exhibition and inspect the splash_screens data.
  2. Verify it references the correct exhibitionID (not a stale or empty ID), confirming init happens inside the transaction.

Regression / build

  • Run go build ./... and confirm src/action/v1/exhibition compiles with no errors.
